creator: fuz@FreeBSD.org
text:
Another thing I just noticed: there seems to be a difference between suspending the system for the first time and for subsequent times.

When I suspend the system for the first time and wake it back up, everything seems to mostly be working, except I get a blank screen or graphics glitches.  I can switch consoles and start or terminate X servers (blindly).

However, if I suspend the system again, something seems to be broken within the whole console subsystem: if I suspend from an X server and wake back up, the system seems to get stuck and no longer responds to console input.  Most importantly, I cannot switch consoles anymore.  However, it still reacts to pressing the power button, which after a while causes an orderly shut down.  Curiously, during shutdown, the screen goes from off to glitches, to normal console output, just before shutting down.

If I suspend the system from the text console for the second time, I instead get an empty screen with just a blinking cursor in the top left corner on wakeup.  Console still works (i.e. I can type, didn't try switching consoles) and I can start another X server, which then also works.  If I switch away from the X server to a separate console, I get graphics glitches on that console instead of output.  And the other consoles do not seem to work.

This seems very weird and confusing to me.
